title: styloglossum pumilum
intro: Styloglossum pumilum, also known as the Antelope Orchid, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. The medicinal properties of this plant include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The Antelope Orchid is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it is used to aid in wound healing and to reduce fever. In terms of herbal preparations, Styloglossum pumilum is often dried and used in infusions or tinctures, although its availability and use are highly restricted due to its endangered status.
